title: oxyceros patulus
intro: Oxyceros patulus, commonly known as the "Bengal Trumpet Vine" or "Bengal Creeper," is a climber plant species native to the tropical forests of the Indian subcontinent. It is valued for its numerous health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antiseptic. The therapeutic actions of Oxyceros patulus include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Oxyceros patulus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like skin irritation, respiratory problems, and anaphylaxis in severe cases. Prolonged use of the plant's extracts can also lead to liver and kidney damage due to its toxic compounds. In addition, improper use of Oxyceros patulus may cause cardiovascular issues, including increased heart rate, blood pressure, and even heart failure. Furthermore, misuse of the plant can also lead to neurological side effects, such as headaches, dizziness, and even seizures in extreme cases.
